concerns.text: Abstract & Intro: The claim 'The strongest system (Gemini-3.1-Pro-Preview) achieves an SAA of only 76.0' is factually unsupported. The paper cites 'arXiv:2605.12882' (a future-dated placeholder) and lists 'Gemini-3.1-Pro' which does not exist in the current model landscape (as of 2024/2025). No primary source or internal data table is cited to verify this specific score. The claim must be backed by a real, verifiable benchmark result or removed.

Abstract & Intro: The claim 'strongest open-source MLLM reaches just 22.5' (Qwen3-VL-235B) is unsupported. The model 'Qwen3-VL-235B' is not a publicly released model version (current versions are Qwen2.5-VL). The specific score '22.5' appears to be hallucinated or derived from a non-existent experiment. Cite the specific table (Table 2) and verify the model name/version against the actual evaluated list in the paper's own Table 2.

Section 3.1 (Document Collection): The claim 'Starting from a corpus of over 100 million raw PDF documents (primarily sourced from Common Crawl...)' lacks a specific citation or link to the dataset snapshot/version used. Common Crawl is a stream, not a static '100M PDF' corpus. The claim overstates the specificity of the data source without a verifiable reference (e.g., a specific Crawl ID or a derived dataset like CCpdf).

Section 3.3 (Quality Control): The claim 'Ground-truth annotation used Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B exclusively' is factually inconsistent with the model name 'Qwen3-VL-235B' which does not exist. If this refers to a specific internal or unreleased model, it must be explicitly defined or the claim must be revised to use a known model version to maintain factual accuracy.

Table 1 (Benchmark Compare): The claim 'ViDoRe V3... 137.0 Avg. Pg.' cites 'loison2026vidore'. The year 2026 is in the future. While this may be a pre-print, the citation format and the specific statistic (137.0 pages) must be verified against the actual published or pre-print version of ViDoRe V3 to ensure the number is not hallucinated.
